'''Terpfen''' was a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] ship mechanic, who served as the personal mechanic of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]].

==Biography==
[[File:Terpfen.jpg|thumb|left|Terpfen (left) discusses the [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]] with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] (right).]]

Terpfen was named after a [[Terpfen (coral)|species of coral]] indigenous to his homeworld.

Terpfen became an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] slave with many of his fellow [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] and was forced to design and perfect Star Destroyers. He had also been brainwashed by Imperial forces on [[Carida/Legends|Carida]] who replaced half his brain with organic implants and forced to live life as a sleeper agent when he returned to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. He managed to [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] Admiral Ackbar's personal [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing]] shuttle, which caused the craft to crash, nearly killing the admiral and his passenger, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]]. He also stole the coordinates for the location of [[Anoth/Legends|Anoth]] and sent them to [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Furgan]].

When Carida was destroyed by [[Kyp Durron]], Terpfen was freed from Imperial control. He was desperate to confess to his sins, so he stole a [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]] from the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and flew to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to warn [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] that Furgan was planning to capture her son, [[Anakin Solo]]. In this escape from Coruscant, Terpfen felt guilty when a pilot died trying to capture him.

It was during a rescue mission to Anoth that Ackbar learned of Terpfen's betrayal. Yet, Terpfen finally overcame the implants in his brain, helping save young [[Anakin Solo]] and killing Furgan. Ridden with guilt, he tried to send his [[Mountain Terrain Armored Transport]] walker over a cliff. Quick action by Ackbar prevented him from taking his life, who said his punishment would be to live.

Upon returning to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Ackbar spoke to [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] about how Terpfen had not been acting of his own accord due to Imperial brainwashing. Mon Mothma agreed with Ackbar's conclusions and granted Terpfen a full pardon.

Shortly after, Terpfen told Ackbar and [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] - who had been suffering from a mysterious and devastating illness over the past several months - that she was not actually suffering from a biological illness. Instead her rapid deterioration was due to poisoning, which was caused by self-replicating nano-destroyers that Furgan had personally infected her with.

Admiral Ackbar convinced Terpfen that he still had much service he could do for the New Republic and the galaxy. Terpfen agreed, deciding to return to [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] to help oversee the rebuilding efforts repairing damage inflicted by rogue admiral [[Natasi Daala|Daala]].
